label: Strategy Classification
definition: Categorization of an entity's business strategy

label: Viability Prediction
definition: Expert prediction of entity's long-term survival or market trajectory

label: Actual Outcome
definition: Verified historical outcome for an entity or technology

label: Technology Assessment
definition: Evaluation of a technology's maturity, fit, or competitive position

label: Benchmark Result
definition: Quantitative comparison against peers or industry standards

label: Framework Factor
definition: One component of a multi-factor analytical framework

label: Market Data
definition: Quantitative market statistic or adoption figure

label: Expert Opinion
definition: Qualitative professional judgment by industry analyst

label: Industry Analysis
definition: Systematic analysis of industry structure and competitive dynamics

label: Competitive Profiling
definition: Detailed comparison of competing entities or strategies

label: Benchmarking
definition: Comparison of performance against industry standards or peers

label: Field Research
definition: Primary data collection via interviews with vendors or customers

label: Document Review
definition: Analysis of existing vendor documentation and announcements

label: Statistical Analysis
definition: Quantitative data analysis with statistical methods

label: High Confidence
definition: Strong evidentiary basis; multiple corroborating sources

label: Medium Confidence
definition: Reasonable basis but limited independent corroboration

label: Low Confidence
definition: Directional judgment with limited evidence

label: Verified
definition: Confirmed by subsequent events or independent historical sources

label: Emerging
definition: Technology newly introduced, market adoption beginning

label: Mature
definition: Technology widely deployed, mainstream market adoption

label: Declining
definition: Technology losing market share to newer alternatives

label: Legacy Supported
definition: Technology no longer mainstream but still maintained by vendor

label: Obsolete
definition: Technology no longer sold or supported

label: Active
definition: Technology currently in active development and deployment
